Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.

end of world model.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
That didn't work either. I'm at 122 actions and still stuck on Level 3. Let me completely rethink the problem.
Looking at the image again, I see:
1. Left panel: dark gray background with a small charcoal shape
2. Right panel: checkerboard with magenta bar and yellow objects
3. Left H/V buttons (toggle)
4. Right H/V buttons (toggle)
5. Preview boxes at bottom (toggle left buttons)
6. Blue octagonal trigger button
The trigger NEVER works. The preview boxes toggle left buttons. The H/V buttons toggle. Nothing completes the level.
Wait - let me look at the image more carefully. I see there are TWO white buttons below the right V buttons. One is at col ~55 and one at col ~61. Let me look at the ascii more carefully.
Actually, looking at the image, I see the white button with black top is BELOW the right V buttons, not to the right. Let me check the full ascii around that area.
Also, I notice the blue octagonal button is at the bottom right. Maybe I need to click it differently?
Actually, let me reconsider the entire game. In Levels 1 and 2:
- Level 1: trigger moved the charcoal shape
- Level 2: trigger completed the level when right V was all OFF
In Level 3, the trigger doesn't work. What if the trigger DOES work but only when the LEFT buttons are in a specific configuration?
Let me try: set left H to all OFF, left V to all OFF, right H to all OFF, right V to all OFF, then trigger.
Or maybe I need to look at what the preview boxes are showing more carefully. They might be showing the TARGET configuration for the right buttons.
Let me look at the preview boxes again:
- Box 1: yellow at bottom-left
- Box 2: yellow at top-right
- Box 3: yellow on left side
- Box 4: yellow on right side
What if these represent which RIGHT buttons should be ON?
Box 1: bottom-left = right V button 1 ON
Box 2: top-right = right H button 6 ON
Box 3: left side = right H buttons 1-3 ON
Box 4: right side = right H buttons 4-6 ON
Let me try setting the right buttons to match one of these patterns and see if the trigger works.
Actually, wait. Let me step back and think about this more carefully. I've been assuming the trigger is the way to complete the level, but what if it's not? What if the goal is to:
1. Set the left and right button configurations to match
2. The level completes when they match
Or what if the goal is to:
1. Set the left buttons to match the right buttons
2. The level completes when they match
Let me try: set left H = right H and left V = right V, and see if the level completes.
Currently:
- Left H: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwwwg (all ON)
- Left V: ggwggggwggggwggggwgg (all ON)
- Right H: wwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwww (all ON)
- Right V: WBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBW (all OFF)
So left H and right H are both ON, but left V is ON and right V is OFF. Let me turn left V OFF and see if the level completes.

- Box 4: cols 31-39 (BbBWBWBYYb) - has 'W' at cols 33,35 and 'Y' at cols 37-38
Wait, the 'W' and 'B' in the preview boxes represent white and black dots. The 'Y' represents yellow regions.
So:
- Box 1: yellow at bottom (rows 60-61), white dots at rows 55-56
- Box 2: yellow at top (rows 55-56), white dots at rows 60-61
- Box 3: yellow on left (cols 23-24), white dots on right (cols 26)

These patterns look like they represent the button configurations!
Box 1: H=OFF (yellow bottom), V=ON (white top) - or vice versa
Box 2: H=ON (yellow top), V=OFF (white bottom)
Box 3: H=ON (yellow left), V=OFF (white right)
Box 4: H=OFF (yellow right), V=ON (white left)
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me try a different approach.

Wait, let me re-examine. The white button is at:

Row 59: ...BgBbBBBBBYYb...
So the white button is at cols 33-37, rows 57-59. It has 'W' (white) centers at cols 34 and 36, with 'B' (black) at col 35.
This is the trigger button! I've been clicking it at (58, 57) but it's actually at (58, 35) or so.
Wait no, I've been clicking at (58, 57) which is the blue octagonal button. The white trigger button is at (58, 35).
Let me try clicking the white trigger button!
